overview: The XML Stream Filter processes XML documents using a **stream parser**, allowing it to handle much larger documents than the DOM-based [XML Filter](XML_Filter). It uses the same YAML-based configuration syntax as the HTML Filter. If you need [ITS](https://www.w3.org/TR/its20/) support, use the XML Filter instead.

inlineCodes.help:
Controls which XML elements are treated as inline codes (formatting tags) within translatable text units rather than as structural elements.

Uses the same inline code configuration as the HTML Filter.

parser.assumeWellformed.help:
Controls whether the parser assumes the input XML is well-formed.

When enabled, the parser skips strict well-formedness checks, which can improve performance on large documents. Disable if you need strict XML validation during processing.

parser.preserveWhitespace.help:
Controls whether whitespace in the XML content is preserved as-is.

When disabled, the filter may normalize whitespace according to standard XML processing rules. Enable this for documents where whitespace is significant (e.g., pre-formatted content).

attributes.help:
Defines which XML attributes should be extracted for translation.

Each key is an attribute name, and the value configures the extraction rule for that attribute. Attributes listed here are extracted globally — across all elements in the document.

Uses the same attribute rule configuration as the HTML Filter.

limitations: No transparent support for namespace prefixes — element names must be declared with their prefix in the configuration.
The filter is **not case-sensitive**: elements like `<elem>` and `<Elem>` are treated as identical, which violates the XML specification.
processingNotes: **Encoding detection**: Uses the document's encoding declaration if present; otherwise defaults to UTF-8 regardless of any specified default encoding.
**BOM handling**: When outputting UTF-8, a BOM is written only if the input was also UTF-8 and had a BOM. If the input was a different encoding, no BOM is added.
**Encoding declaration**: If the original document had an XML encoding declaration, it is updated on output; if not, one is automatically added.
**Line breaks**: Output line-break style matches the original input document.
**CDATA subfiltering**: CDATA sections can be processed with an additional filter (e.g., `okf_html`) via the `global_cdata_subfilter` YAML parameter.

**PCDATA subfiltering**: Escaped markup in PCDATA (e.g., double-escaped HTML) can be processed with a subfilter via `global_pcdata_subfilter`. Only content matched by a `TEXTUNIT` tag rule is passed to the subfilter — `INCLUDE` rules are excluded.
All parameters from the HTML Filter are available except `escapeCharacters`.

examples.title: CDATA subfiltering with HTML
examples.description: Process CDATA sections containing HTML markup with the HTML filter as a subfilter.

examples.title: PCDATA subfiltering with escaped HTML
examples.description: Process double-escaped HTML content in PCDATA. The element containing the escaped HTML must be configured with a `TEXTUNIT` rule for the subfilter to apply.
examples.input:
```xml
<test>
  <p>This is embedded HTML content within a p tag.</p>
</test>
```

examples.title: Custom quote escaping mode
examples.description: Configure how quotes and apostrophes are escaped in the output. Mode 3 escapes only double quotes.
